vision: Deliver a resilient, AI-native IT software estate for Centrica that balances cost, sovereignty, ESG and innovation — while removing single-vendor concentration risk and meeting OFGEM RIIO-3 supply-chain assurance.

objectives.objective: Reduce hyperscaler concentration from 68% to 55% of category spend
Eliminate certification compliance gaps across PSL
Close Scope 3 reporting gap for IT Software supply chain
Achieve £1.6m annualised savings through renewal benchmarking
Embed AI-pricing-cap clauses in all GenAI renewals

porters.title: Porter's Five Forces — IT Software (Buyer perspective)

forces.assessment: Cloud-native challengers (Snowflake, Atlassian) entering steadily. Open-source ERP (Odoo) and CRM (SuiteCRM) becoming credible at the margins. Yet hyperscaler distribution moats remain formidable.
forces.implication: Maintain a 'challenger scout' brief — Snowflake onboarding (2025) showed value. Test 1 net-new vendor per FY.

forces.assessment: Centrica is a meaningful FTSE 100 buyer (~£23m IT software spend) but switching cost across Microsoft / SAP / Oracle is structurally high. Joint procurement with other UK utilities could shift the balance.
forces.implication: Aggregate demand with British Gas, Bord Gáis, Hive. Explore CIPS UK utilities buying consortium pilot.

forces.assessment: Three vendors hold 68% of spend. Pricing inflation runs 7–12% YoY. GenAI add-ons (Copilot, Agentforce, Now Assist) introduce new per-seat / per-conversation cost vectors. Oracle audit posture remains aggressive.
forces.implication: Multi-cloud, multi-suite by design. Embed price-cap and rate-card clauses in every renewal.

forces.assessment: Open-source alternatives strengthening (PostgreSQL vs Oracle DB; Odoo vs SAP for non-core; Mattermost vs Teams in OT). LLM-built micro-apps replacing some low-code use cases.
forces.implication: Test open-source substitution where regulatory and OT constraints permit. Build in-house GenAI tooling for narrow use cases.

forces.assessment: Hyperscaler price war reshaped by GenAI investment cycle. CRM / ITSM / HCM increasingly converging — multiple suites pitching for the same workflows.
forces.implication: Exploit competitive tension at every renewal. Refuse single-source RFPs for any spend >£250k.

strengths: Mature category-management discipline (cat-plan reviewed every 9 months)
Single, governed PSL — 9 vendors covering ≥95% of spend
Group scale — joint procurement leverage across British Gas / Bord Gáis / Hive
weaknesses: 68% spend concentration in three hyperscalers — structurally high switching cost
5 of 27 certifications expired or expiring within 90 days
Limited GenAI commercial expertise — pricing models still maturing
Slow renewal cycles — 3 out of 8 contracts running on rolling extensions
opportunities: Consolidate fragmented dev-tooling spend (Atlassian, GitHub, GitLab)
Leverage Microsoft EA scale for Copilot at concession pricing
S/4HANA bundle commercial leverage with SAP via RISE
Net-new vendor scout (Q2 FY27) → bring in 1 disruptor per segment
threats: EU AI Act compliance burden on GenAI tooling
OFGEM RIIO-3 IT supply-chain attestation (2027) — non-trivial evidence collection
Hyperscaler pricing inflation outpacing IT inflation index
Activist-investor activity at Pega — strategic continuity risk

kraljic.title: Kraljic Matrix — IT Software vendors
kraljic.description: Vendor classification by spend exposure (vertical) and supply-side risk (horizontal). Drives differentiated sourcing approach.

quadrants.name: Strategic
quadrants.subtitle: High spend · High risk
quadrants.approach: Partnership · joint roadmap · multi-year commercial · executive sponsorship

quadrants.name: Leverage
quadrants.subtitle: High spend · Low risk
quadrants.approach: Competitive tendering · price-driven · multi-source · benchmark every renewal

quadrants.name: Bottleneck
quadrants.subtitle: Low spend · High risk
quadrants.approach: Develop alternatives · stockpile evidence · contingency planning

quadrants.name: Routine
quadrants.subtitle: Low spend · Low risk
quadrants.approach: Simplify · automate · consume via marketplace where possible

sourcing_strategy.approach: Multi-source by design with strategic depth on the top 3

allocation_targets.target: Microsoft 80% / GCP or AWS 20% by FY28

allocation_targets.rationale: Strategic lock-in justified by integration depth

allocation_targets.target: Pega 60% / Salesforce Service Cloud 40% by FY28
